channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court reinstates order for the Trump administration to un-freeze foreign aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sharply divided Supreme Court on Wednesday reinstated a lower-court order for the Trump administration to release frozen foreign aid, but it was not clear how quickly money would start flowing.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Businesses scramble to contain fallout from Trump’s tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico
item.description: A Minnesota farmer worries about the price of fertilizer. A San Diego entrepreneur deals with an unexpected increase in the cost of remodeling a restaurant. A Midwestern sheet metal fabricator bemoans the prospect of higher aluminum prices.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Read NPR’s annotated fact check of President Trump’s address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump vowed to keep up his campaign of “swift and unrelenting action” in reorienting the nation’s economy, immigration and foreign policy as he delivered an unyielding address Tuesday night to Congress and the nation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Al Green shouts down Trump and may face censure by the House for the outburst
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic Rep. Al Green of Texas wasn’t the first lawmaker ever to blurt out a shout of protest during a presidential address to Congress. But he's perhaps the only one in recent memory to actually be ejected from the hall Tuesday night by the Speaker of the House.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy wants peace, ready to accept minerals deal after WH blowup
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Tuesday that Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy had written to him to say he appreciates U.S. support for his country in its war with Russia and is ready to sign a deal that could ensure future American support.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fired federal workers are among the guests invited to Trump's speech to Congress
item.description: The Trumps and lawmakers from across the aisle have invited guests to Tuesday's joint address, representing some of the administration's top priorities. Here's a look at some of the names on the list.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Here's how Trump's tariffs could cost you and your wallet
item.description: Trump has slapped 25 percent tariffs on goods from Canada and Mexico — and an additional 10 percent tax on Chinese goods. Americans are likely to see higher prices on a wide variety of products as a result.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Wamsley, MPR News

item.title: Vance's comments about European peacekeeping plan for Ukraine draw ire abroad
item.description: Vice President Vance said the best security guarantee for Ukraine is to sign a mineral deal with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Linda McMahon has been confirmed as Trump’s secretary of education
item.description: The White House has been clear that it intends to dismantle the U.S. Department of Education, and that it will be McMahon’s job to oversee that effort.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny , Cory Turner, and Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA Times to give op-eds AI-generated political rating, list of alternate views
item.description: The Los Angeles Times has launched an AI-driven "bias meter" to provide readers with alternative perspectives on opinion content.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Canada and China retaliate after Trump’s tariffs take effect
item.description: Canada and China hit back swiftly after Trump’s sweeping tariffs took effect Tuesday, raising fears of a global trade war. International markets, meanwhile, continued to slide.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid and Rebecca Rosman, MPR News

item.title: Nevada film tax bill would bring jobs, but questions remain as lawmakers examine details
item.description: Lawmakers dug into details of the film tax credit bill on Thursday, with film studio executives from Warner Bros. Discovery and Sony answering questions in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawmakers differ over location of Florida’s Black history museum
item.description: Florida has a rich and long history involving contributions from its Black residents. There are areas across the state highlighting those struggles and efforts. Some of those key moments are displayed at the Robert W Saunders, Sr Public Library in Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa Bay families travel to Capitol Hill to urge lawmakers for more pediatric cancer funding
item.description: Embracing photos and wearing the names of their children who were taken too soon, families took off from Tampa International Airport Wednesday morning, headed for D.C. to urge lawmakers to pass more funding for pediatric cancer research.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC
